name: Ahmed
manager_notes: Ahmed talks about senior scope but often redirects into tactical delivery details. I want to understand whether this is temporary pressure or a deeper confidence gap around visibility and influence at his level. This growth conversation should surface how he defines the next-level role, what stakeholder trust gaps he sees, and where he is still operating as an individual executor. I need him to leave with a concrete growth path tied to strategic ownership, not another vague list of good habits.
answers: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
I want to own one strategic bet end-to-end and set a milestone check-in with stakeholders in two weeks.
